summary: Israel halted a planned large-scale air strike on Iran hours before launch, after US pressure. Israeli special forces destroyed 18 weapon caches in the West Bank's Shomron area. Iran's vice president reaffirmed control of the Strait of Hormuz amid rising tensions.
key_points: Israel's Air Force cancelled a broad strike on hundreds of Iranian targets just before takeoff, following US President Trump's instruction (Source: @manniefabian/58608).
Yahelam unit and reserve forces eliminated 18 weapon‑making caches in Blatha and Ras al‑Ain within the Shomron Brigade area during a night operation (Source: @New_security8200/201913).
Iran's foreign ministry warned of a "hard response" from its army if Israel continues attacks in southern Lebanon (Source: @N12chat/213189).
Israel's cabinet declared it will not withdraw from Lebanon and will maintain full operational freedom in the south (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73884).
Iranian Vice President stated Tehran will continue to control the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @presstv/194381).
US President Donald Trump instructed Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu not to escalate the conflict with Iran, leading to the strike cancellation (Source: @manniefabian/58608).
US and Iran discussed a memorandum of understanding that includes a $300 billion private investment fund, though unrelated to immediate combat operations (Source: @asafroz/50396).

sections.executive_summary: Israel halted a large‑scale air strike on Iran after US President Trump warned against escalation. Israeli Yahelam forces destroyed multiple weapon caches in the West Bank, reducing militant capabilities. Iran reaffirmed its strategic control of the Strait of Hormuz, signaling continued regional tension.

points: Iranian Vice President announced Tehran will maintain control of the strategic Strait of Hormuz, underscoring the country's resolve to protect vital maritime routes (Source: @presstv/194381).

Israeli Air Force chief Maj. Gen. Omer Tischler confirmed a planned wave of strikes on Iran was called off an hour before departure, after US President Trump advised against escalation (Source: @manniefabian/58608).

Yahelam unit and reserve battalion 967 conducted a night operation in the Shomron Brigade, destroying 18 weapon‑making caches in Blatha and Ras al‑Ain, seizing additional arms components (Source: @New_security8200/201913).
Israel's cabinet stated the military will not retreat from Lebanon and retains full operational freedom in the south to prevent Hezbollah proximity to the border (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73884).
Iran's foreign ministry warned of a "hard response" from its army if Israel continues its strikes in southern Lebanon, indicating potential escalation (Source: @N12chat/213189).
Israel's cabinet reiterated that Israeli forces will not withdraw from Lebanon and will act against any threat to Israeli territory (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73884).

key_developments: Israel cancelled a broad air strike on Iran hours before launch after US President Trump warned against escalation (Source: @manniefabian/58608).
Yahelam unit eliminated 18 weapon‑making caches in Blatha and Ras al‑Ain, Shomron Brigade, reducing militant firepower (Source: @New_security8200/201913).
Iran warned of a hard military response if Israel persists with attacks in southern Lebanon (Source: @N12chat/213189).
Israel's cabinet declared no withdrawal from Lebanon and full operational freedom in the south (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73884).
Iranian Vice President reaffirmed Tehran's control over the Strait of Hormuz (Source: @presstv/194381).
US President Trump instructed Netanyahu not to escalate the Israel‑Iran confrontation, prompting strike cancellation (Source: @manniefabian/58608).
US and Iran discussed a $300 billion private investment fund as part of a memorandum of understanding (Source: @asafroz/50396).
sections.threat_assessment: The abrupt cancellation of Israel's planned air campaign against Iran indicates a high level of strategic caution, limiting immediate escalation but preserving the threat of future strikes. Iran's explicit warning of a hard response to Israeli actions in southern Lebanon raises the risk of a broader regional confrontation involving Hezbollah. Continued US diplomatic pressure on Israel serves as a moderating factor, yet the presence of sizable weapon caches in the West Bank suggests persistent insurgent capabilities that could fuel further instability.
sections.regional_response: Israel's cabinet stance of no withdrawal from Lebanon signals a commitment to maintain pressure on Hezbollah, while Iran's threat language suggests readiness to retaliate if strikes continue. The United States, through direct instructions to Israel, appears to prioritize de‑escalation, balancing support for Israeli security with concerns over a wider Middle‑East war. Gulf states remain observers, with no explicit actions reported in the current data.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian air defense degradation from the aborted Israeli strikes is essential, as any future attempts may encounter reduced capability. The destruction of 18 weapon caches in the West Bank reduces immediate militant capacity but does not eliminate the threat of re‑stocking. Diplomatic channels between the US, Israel, and Iran regarding the $300 billion fund could influence future economic leverage and affect conflict dynamics.
